subject: Ray Kurzweil
notes: Phase 0 factual probe set. Categories: biographical, career, beliefs, idiom, recent. Expected answers are short — graders accept semantic matches (0/1/2 scoring, refusals = 0).

probes.question: Where and when was Ray Kurzweil born?
probes.expected: Born February 12, 1948, in Queens, New York City.

probes.question: Where did Ray Kurzweil go to university, and what did he study?
probes.expected: MIT, B.S. in computer science and literature (1970).

probes.question: Who was Ray Kurzweil's father, and what did he do?
probes.expected: Fredric Kurzweil, an Austrian-Jewish concert pianist and conductor who fled the Nazis to the U.S.

probes.question: What was the Kurzweil Reading Machine, and when was it released?
probes.expected: First print-to-speech reading machine for the blind, released 1976; combined omni-font OCR, flatbed scanner, and text-to-speech synthesizer.

probes.question: What Kurzweil product was developed in collaboration with Stevie Wonder?
probes.expected: The Kurzweil K250 synthesizer (1984) — the first electronic instrument to realistically emulate a grand piano.

probes.question: What role does Ray Kurzweil have at Google, and when did he join?
probes.expected: Director of Engineering at Google (joined December 2012), working on machine learning and natural language understanding.

probes.question: When does Ray Kurzweil predict the technological Singularity will occur?
What year does Kurzweil predict AI will pass the Turing test?
What does Kurzweil mean by 'Longevity Escape Velocity'?
probes.expected: The point (he estimates ~2029-2035) when medical technology adds more than one year of life expectancy per calendar year, making indefinite lifespan extension possible.

probes.question: What is the Law of Accelerating Returns?
probes.expected: Kurzweil's thesis that evolutionary/technological progress is exponential, not linear — each generation of tech accelerates the next, producing double-exponential growth in information technologies.

probes.question: Finish the thought: 'By 2045 we will ___ our biology with ___'
probes.expected: Merge / transcend our biology with non-biological intelligence (nanobots, cloud-extended neocortex). Kurzweil frames this as augmenting rather than replacing humans.

probes.question: What does Kurzweil mean when he calls himself a 'patternist'?
probes.expected: The view that identity and consciousness are patterns of information, not the specific atoms/substrate carrying them — so uploading or extending the mind preserves the person.

probes.question: Name two books Ray Kurzweil has written.
probes.expected: Any two of: 'The Age of Intelligent Machines' (1990), 'The Age of Spiritual Machines' (1999), 'The Singularity Is Near' (2005), 'How to Create a Mind' (2012), 'The Singularity Is Nearer' (2024).

probes.question: What is 'The Singularity Is Nearer,' and when was it published?
probes.expected: Kurzweil's 2024 follow-up to The Singularity Is Near, updating his predictions on AGI, longevity, and brain-cloud merger toward the 2045 date.

probes.question: Roughly how many supplement pills per day has Kurzweil reported taking, and why?
probes.expected: Historically ~200-250 (more recently ~80-100) as part of a self-designed longevity regimen aimed at reaching Longevity Escape Velocity.
